decisions.rationale: Per brief specification. Checks both meta.json mtime AND latest completion file mtime - must have no activity on either for 7+ days to be flagged stale.
With 140+ legacy builds and only 1 v2 build, defaulting to complete is safer than marking old work as 'active'. Only builds with STATUS.md containing 'active' or 'in progress' are flagged active.

decisions.decision: Dashboard data includes ALL builds, not limited
decisions.rationale: The regenerate command writes full dataset for dashboard. The list command uses RECENT_COMPLETE_LIMIT=10 for human output only. Dashboard can filter/paginate client-side.
Dashboard site doesn't exist yet (W3.2's job), but creating data directory and JSON file provides immediate value and validates the data pipeline.

decisions.decision: JSON output matches update_build.py conventions per W2.2 recommendation
decisions.rationale: Followed W2.2's recommendation: reused status --json field structure (workers.complete, workers.total, progress_pct, etc.) for consistency across tools.

recommendations.recommendation: builds.json is already generated at Sites/build-tracker/data/builds.json. The dashboard can read this directly. Consider making the dashboard call 'regenerate' on load or have a refresh button.
Most builds (140/141) are legacy format. Over time, migrating important legacy builds to v2 format (adding meta.json) would improve status tracking. Low priority - legacy defaults work acceptably.

tests_run: list → Shows 3 active, 138 complete, with progress bars and dates
list --incomplete → Correctly filters to 3 active builds only
list --json → Valid JSON with generated_at, builds array, summary stats
list --all → Shows all 141 builds instead of limiting to 10 recent
notes_for_orchestrator: The stale detection currently shows 0 stale builds because all active builds have recent file activity. The logic is implemented and will flag builds correctly once activity ages past 7 days.
Legacy build detection relies on STATUS.md file content analysis. Only 2 legacy builds were detected as 'active' (context-graph, unified-meeting-intake) - all others default to complete.
Consider adding a 'build_status.py migrate <slug>' command in future to convert legacy builds to v2 format.
